It's okay. It's a 12 month or it's a 24 month or it's a 36 month program and at the end of that period of time It automatically renews and we take a pre-agreed upon money again The stuff you're most commonly seeing in our world is the till forbid pricing formula where it's so much a month typically few people are so much a week Steve at Oliver is a twice a month charge So it's so much twice a month Charged on the first and the 15th and it is continuity till forbid you got to scream you got to get out There's the a lot of the traditional coaching is one-on-one delivery meaning you're paying It may be structured by a program, but you're paying for Entirely or partially the right to talk to me on the phone personally to send me emails to send me faxes And or even to show up at my door and sit and talk to me I have in most of the coaching stuff. I've done I have had always some one-on-one phone activity Some people do it once and decide never to do it again because they find it too painful And I understand But it is a big value builder for people it really adds to the value of the program and it is It is a way to actually really help people The ones who use it properly so I've almost always had it every coaching program has some version of You got a time slot and you call and we talk Now just so you know about that Just as because people just as people are in coaching programs for different reasons Their behavior in coaching is very different from one person or another and so in one-on-one coaching You wind up with a lot of individual by individual by individual variants that doesn't surface as much in a group environment So some people call totally unprepared for the call they haven't given a thought before they jumped on the phone and They want to talk about the weather and who do you think's gonna win the football game this Sunday? And they use up some of the time half of the time all the time doing that Some people call the exact opposite they're like Thoroughly prepared they've sent in the list ahead of time if here's the five things I want to talk about Here's the order in which I want to talk about a man. If so if we don't get to number five That's okay. We'll read the three here's the three or four facts. You need to know about each one of those And man there I'm picking up the phone and they're on number two, you know Okay, and and and that's fine some people are calling for therapy All right. It's really not a business call at all. It's their kids their wife their employees the Their mother-in-law the relative they're supporting you know Now that people don't I got a dog got one guy that says can be dog questions, you know You know I'm going I don't know I let mine do whatever it wants. That's my solution to you know Some people are in my case very marketing oriented some people the edges are very fuzzy Some people call it for accountability. Some people don't want accountability. Some people call it for recognition Some people don't want recognition Some people miss their calls a little some people miss their calls a lot Different people have different philosophies about that by the way some coaching programs The coach calls the person at a prescribed time I've never done that but there are coaching programs to do that some coaches if somebody misses a call they chase them Some coaches if somebody misses a call they'll give them a make-up call personally. I don't do anything You ain't there tough Then there's from multiple individuals meaning group delivery now that's the Certainly, it's the most efficient. Oh the other thing I should tell you about one-on-one calls is if you do do a fair amount of them My advice to you is find like some way to Like be entertained while you're doing it Because after a while just like staying with it can sometimes be a problem So first of all I told you be careful about the order you put people in so you know if you got a few problem children You don't want like four problem children one right after the other because then you're gonna be irritable But like when the weather's good, I like to do I like to sit outside part of the time on the deck and you know I'll put the TV on with no sound and watch a couple horse races while I'm talking I'll sort mail
